people_management.headline_findings: This vocabulary list is NOT trustworthy as currently constructed: roughly half of all flagged postings will be false positives driven by four polluting tokens. 'supervised' (n=131) is dominantly the ML term 'supervised learning'; 'supervision' (n=648) is overwhelmingly the autonomy phrase 'minimal/limited supervision' (i.e., the OPPOSITE meaning); 'oversee/oversight/oversees' (n=1,048 combined) is mostly project/process oversight rather than people; and 'team of' (n=1,527) catches 'collaborate with a team of' / 'join a team of' boilerplate. The 'Coaching, mentoring, and career development' concept (24% hit rate) is the largest concept by volume but conflates informal mentorship and learning-and-development benefits with the people-management responsibilities the topic definition explicitly tries to isolate. The high-precision signals (direct reports, engineering manager, people management/leader, weekly 1:1, performance evaluations/appraisals/reviews, compensation recommendations, manage/managing a team of, hiring decisions, headcount, Tech-Lead-Manager) are present but drowned out and need to become the load-bearing terms for the measurement. After the fixes below the list should be usable; without them, every reported people-mgmt rate will be inflated by an order of magnitude.

drops.reason: Zero hits; covered by broader 'will report to you' / 'direct reports'.
Zero hits at this regex literal; the matcher likely treats it as literal text. Replace with explicit numeric anchors (see ADDs).
Dominantly 'supervised learning' (ML term); 'supervised and unsupervised'; almost zero people-management usage in examples.
Examples are 'supervising performance and quality assurance processes' and 'supervising and updating predictive models' — process supervision, not people.
Examples are 'oversee implementations', 'oversee the cloud systems', 'oversee the development of our website' — project/system oversight, not people.
Examples are 'oversees change control processes', 'oversees test-plan development', 'oversees deployment' — process oversight, not people.
Examples are 'oversight of the quality review', 'oversight of developers to provide accurate estimates' (work oversight, not people management) — project/QA framing dominates.
Dominantly 'minimal supervision' / 'limited supervision' / 'without supervision' — connotes IC autonomy, the OPPOSITE of people management. Catastrophic false positive.
Examples are 'reporting to the supervisor', 'directed by your supervisor' — the candidate is the subordinate, not the manager.
Catches 'collaborate with a team of', 'join a team of', 'mentor a team of' — most are not management. The manager-only patterns are already captured by 'manage/managing/lead/leading a team of'.
Topic definition explicitly excludes informal mentorship; 'mentor' fires on 13% of all postings as a generic senior-IC duty ('mentor others on best practices').
Same as 'mentor' — informal mentorship by ICs, not people management.
Past-tense informal mentorship; not a people-management signal per topic definition.
Excluded by topic definition (informal mentorship). Fires on 7.5% of postings — almost entirely senior-IC mentorship.
Same as 'mentoring' — fires on senior-IC roles offering 'guidance and mentorship to development teams', not management.
Generic senior-IC verb ('coach team members on best practices'); also catches 'player/coach' substring. Excluded by topic definition.
Plural noun/verb; matches 'coaches technology communities' which is generic IC influencing, not people management.

guards.false_positive_pattern: 'no supervisory responsibilities' / 'does not have supervisory responsibilities' (negation) appears in JD boilerplate (e.g., Application Support Specialist, Solutions Architect/Project Manager).
guards.suggested_guard: Add a negation guard: drop matches within 30 chars of 'no '/'does not have '/'without' preceding the term.

guards.false_positive_pattern: 'limited supervision and may supervise/lead others' — conditional/optional framing. Also 'supervise others' is fine but rare without context.
guards.suggested_guard: Require co-occurrence with a people noun ('staff'|'team members'|'engineers'|'developers'|'employees'|'reports') within 8 tokens; drop bare 'supervise'.

guards.false_positive_pattern: 'supervises the design, development, and maintenance' / 'supervises change control processes' — process not people.
guards.suggested_guard: Require people object: 'supervises ' + ('staff'|'engineers'|'team'|'employees'|'developers'|'analysts'|'reports').

guards.false_positive_pattern: 'project team leader' as a project-management role; 'may assume the role of team leader as assigned' is rotational/informal — and it often refers to OTHER people in the org.
guards.suggested_guard: Require first/second-person framing ('as a team leader you', 'serve as team leader of') OR people-management cooccurrence; drop bare third-party mentions.

guards.false_positive_pattern: Plural form mostly refers to OTHER people the candidate collaborates with: 'collaborate with team leads', 'partner with product owners, tech leads, designers' — not the role itself.
guards.suggested_guard: Drop, or require possessive/role framing ('as team leads', 'team leads will'); plural form is dominated by collaborator references.

guards.false_positive_pattern: Often a senior-IC technical-leadership title with no people-management responsibility ('Senior engineer / Tech Lead', 'Experience as a mentor, tech lead or leading an engineering team' — listed as alternatives).
guards.suggested_guard: Treat 'tech lead' as ambiguous/technical-leadership only; either drop from people-management or require people-mgmt cooccurrence (direct reports / 1:1 / hire / performance review) within the same paragraph.

guards.false_positive_pattern: Plural form is collaborator reference ('partner with product owners, tech leads, engineers'); not the candidate role.
guards.suggested_guard: Drop or require role-defining context.

guards.false_positive_pattern: Senior-IC title used interchangeably with 'mentor' — explicitly listed as separate from people management ('Act as a Technical Lead AND mentor junior engineers'). High volume (n=338) makes this the largest false-positive contributor in this concept.
guards.suggested_guard: Drop from people-management vocabulary; if retained, require people-mgmt context (direct reports / 1:1 / hire / fire / review) in same posting.

guards.false_positive_pattern: Plural form refers to collaborators or peer roles ('partner with technical leads', 'mentor technical leads').
guards.suggested_guard: Drop.

guards.false_positive_pattern: Frequently the candidate REPORTS TO an engineering lead ('reporting to the Data Engineering Lead', 'supporting the Engineering Lead') — i.e., the candidate is the subordinate.
guards.suggested_guard: Drop matches preceded by 'report(ing|s) to', 'supporting', 'work(ing) with'.

guards.false_positive_pattern: Plural collaborator reference.
guards.suggested_guard: Drop.

guards.false_positive_pattern: Senior-IC title; 'You will report to a Lead Engineer'; 'Principal Engineer will be their lead engineer, designing and integrating' — IC framing dominates.
guards.suggested_guard: Drop from people-management vocabulary; this is a technical-leadership / staff-IC role marker.

guards.false_positive_pattern: Senior-IC title with no implied people management ('be an architect and lead developer who will transform pieces of silicon').
guards.suggested_guard: Drop.

guards.false_positive_pattern: Job-title prefix in the SWE labeling itself; 'As a Lead Software Engineer, you will be responsible for administering...' — duties are technical, not managerial.
guards.suggested_guard: Drop. The title alone is too noisy; people management for these requires explicit people-mgmt language elsewhere in the JD.

guards.false_positive_pattern: Often used as a third-party reference ('as directed by team lead'); also frequently an IC senior label.
guards.suggested_guard: Require role-defining context: 'as the team lead', 'as a team lead' OR people-mgmt cooccurrence.

guards.false_positive_pattern: Frequently a soft-skill listing ('Team leadership skills.') without explicit people-mgmt duties.
guards.suggested_guard: Acceptable but weak; consider weighting lower or requiring it to co-fire with another concept hit before counting.

guards.false_positive_pattern: Often a benefit offered to the candidate ('opportunities for career growth') rather than a duty to grow others.
guards.suggested_guard: Require possessive 'their/team's/employees'/your team's' or 'foster X career growth'; drop bare 'career growth' in benefits sections.

guards.false_positive_pattern: Mixed: sometimes a duty ('invest in the career development of your employees'), sometimes a benefit ('career development - internal mobility, mentoring opportunities').
guards.suggested_guard: Require the phrase 'career development of'/'their career development'/'your team's career development'.

guards.false_positive_pattern: Almost always a benefit ('opportunities for professional development') or self-development ('maintains professional growth and development through seminars').
guards.suggested_guard: Drop or require 'professional development of [their|your team|engineers|reports]'.

guards.false_positive_pattern: Same as 'professional development' — mostly a self-development phrase.
guards.suggested_guard: Drop or require possessive direction toward subordinates.

guards.false_positive_pattern: Often the candidate's own progression ('career progression from Software Engineer to the Architect level').
guards.suggested_guard: Require co-occurrence with 'team members'/'reports'/'your team'/'engineers' within same sentence.

guards.false_positive_pattern: Catches 'performance management infrastructure', 'capacity management and performance management tools', 'IT performance management' — system performance, not people performance.
guards.suggested_guard: Add disqualifier: drop if preceded by ('system'|'application'|'database'|'capacity'|'IT'|'cloud'|'tool'|'asset').

guards.false_positive_pattern: Catches 'monthly reports and quarterly performance reviews for the customer' and 'database performance reviews' — non-people uses appear.
guards.suggested_guard: Drop matches preceded by ('database'|'system'|'application'|'customer'|'project'|'campaign').

guards.false_positive_pattern: Catches '1:1 financial planners', '1:1 basis' (regex/equivalence), '1:1 mentoring', '1:1 feature implementations' — mostly NOT one-on-one meetings.
guards.suggested_guard: Require '1:1 meeting'/'1:1s with'/'weekly 1:1' phrasing; drop bare '1:1' which is overwhelmingly off-topic.

guards.false_positive_pattern: Frequently appears in legally-required salary-disclosure boilerplate ('Compensation decisions depend on a wide range of factors...') — not a duty of the candidate.
guards.suggested_guard: Require active-voice context: 'make compensation decisions', 'own compensation decisions', 'responsible for compensation decisions'.

guards.false_positive_pattern: Sometimes describes the product the company is building ('compensation bands... compensation planning' — fintech/HR product descriptions).
guards.suggested_guard: Require manager-duty context: 'lead/responsible for/conduct compensation planning'.

adds.evidence_snippet: 'Demonstrated people-management experience leading small engineering/data teams.' (Director, Principal AI Engineer, scraped 2026-04). Currently 'people-management' hyphenated form gets only 4 hits because the multi-word phrase 'people-management experience' is more specific.
Useful for explicit role identification; complements bare 'engineering manager' which can appear as collaborator reference. Seen in 'as a hands-on Engineering Manager'.

'Conduct regular performance discussions, promotion assessments, and compensation recommendations grounded in impact' (Manager, Mobile Development, scraped 2026-04). High-precision people-mgmt phrase not currently captured.
Same Manager, Mobile Development snippet — promotion-decision authority is a strong people-mgmt marker missing from the list.
Natural variant; complements 'compensation decisions' for parallel coverage of promotion authority.
'Partner with talent acquisition to hire and onboard new team members' (Director, Principal AI Engineer, scraped 2026-04). Captures explicit hiring action by the role.
'Contribute to recruiting and interviewing efforts as we scale the team' (Senior Full Stack Engineer, scraped 2026-04). Also 'Participate in recruiting & interviewing other, future engineers'.

'Managing 3-5 engineers who will report to you and providing them career direction' (Senior II Software Engineer Lead, scraped 2026-04). Distinct from 'career development' and is unambiguously manager-to-report.
Replacement for the broken 'team of \d+' pattern; matches numeric team-size disclosures like 'team of 5 engineers', 'team of 12'. Found in many scraped postings (e.g., 'Managing 3-5 engineers').

concept_redefines.issue: This concept is the highest-volume in the topic (24% hit rate, n=4,640) but its top keywords (mentor 13%, mentoring 7.5%, mentorship 3.8%, coach 2.1%) are explicitly excluded by the topic definition as informal mentorship / senior-IC behavior. As constructed, this concept measures senior-IC duties, not people management.
concept_redefines.suggested_fix: Either (a) drop the entire concept from people-management and move it under a separate 'senior-IC mentorship' topic, or (b) narrow to manager-only phrasings: 'career development of [their|your team]', 'develop talent', 'talent development', 'grow engineers' (with possessive), 'develop your team', 'grow your team', 'individual development plan'. Drop bare mentor*/coach* tokens entirely.

concept_redefines.issue: Three of the highest-volume keywords are net-negative for the topic: 'supervised' is dominantly 'supervised learning' (ML term), 'supervision' is dominantly 'minimal supervision' (autonomy framing — opposite meaning), and 'oversee/oversight' is dominantly process oversight. The concept as a whole has a 10.5% hit rate but most of those are false positives.
concept_redefines.suggested_fix: Split into (a) 'Direct supervisory verbs' = supervise/manage with required people-object cooccurrence and (b) drop oversee/oversight/supervision/supervised entirely from people-management (these are project-leadership / ML / autonomy phrases). After the prune, expected hit rate drops to ~1-2% but precision rises sharply.

concept_redefines.issue: Concept conflates IC-senior titles ('lead software engineer', 'technical lead', 'lead engineer', 'lead developer') with actual team-leadership phrasings. The IC titles dominate by volume (lead software engineer n=294, technical lead n=338, tech lead n=181) and most are pure technical-leadership roles without people-management duties. The topic definition acknowledges 'tech-lead/lead-engineer hybrid roles that imply some people responsibility' but the keyword list does not enforce the 'imply some people responsibility' part.
concept_redefines.suggested_fix: Split into (a) 'Title-only team-lead markers' (team lead, team leader, head of engineering) treated as WEAK signals requiring confirmation from another concept hit, and (b) 'Action-verb team leadership' (lead a team of, leading a team of, manage a team of, managing a team of, led a team of) as STRONG signals. Move all 'lead software engineer'/'lead developer'/'lead engineer'/'technical lead'/'tech lead'/'engineering lead' OUT of people-management and into a separate 'technical leadership' topic.

concept_redefines.issue: 'team of' alone is far too generic; it captures collaborator/peer-team references, not management scope. Only the verb-anchored variants ('manage/managing/lead/leading a team of') signal management.
concept_redefines.suggested_fix: Drop bare 'team of'. Keep only verb-anchored variants and explicit numeric/size phrasings (team size, span of control, headcount of, team of [N]). Fix the broken regex 'team of \d+' (currently zero hits) — replace with proper regex pattern.

concept_redefines.issue: Bare '1:1' catches non-meeting uses ('1:1 financial planners', '1:1 basis', '1:1 feature implementations'); concept hit rate is already low (0.2%) so it does not poison the topic, but it adds noise.
concept_redefines.suggested_fix: Replace bare '1:1' with phrase forms only: '1:1 meeting(s)', '1:1s with', 'weekly 1:1', 'regular 1:1'. Keep one-on-one(s) as-is.

orchestration.headline_findings: The list is structurally strong but contaminated by several high-volume tokens that fire on entirely unrelated meanings: 'MCP' is dominantly a Marketing Cloud / Microsoft Certified Professional / Mariner Credentialing acronym in 2024 data, 'HITL' is overwhelmingly Hardware-in-the-Loop (not Human-in-the-Loop), and 'A2A' frequently means B2B-style Application-to-Application integration. The Agent Memory concept is severely polluted: 'memory management', 'memory system', 'memory systems', and 'short-term memory' all match C/C++ memory management and DDR/LSTM hardware/ML contexts and have nothing to do with agent state. The 'architect' / 'architects' tokens fire mostly on job titles (Java Architect, ServiceNow Architect) rather than the activity of architecting, inflating Software Architecture's hit rate; the inflected verb forms ('architecting', 'architected') are clean. Genuine emerging-practice signals (agent harnesses, agentic primitives, sub-agents, supervisor-worker, spec-driven, CLAUDE.md) are all real but extremely rare, which is a substantive finding rather than a list defect; the 2026-04 stratum is doing the work for those. Several useful agent-era keywords are missing from snippets: 'Bedrock AgentCore', 'Strands Agents', 'AgentCore', 'Foundry agents', 'Strands', 'planner/executor', 'tool dispatch', 'agentic loops', 'Agent Core', 'Glean', 'ACP', 'Codex'.

drops.reason: Acronym collides heavily with Salesforce Marketing Cloud Personalization, Microsoft Certified Professional, Mariner Credentialing Program, and Mission Control Panel; the 2024 hits are overwhelmingly non-agent. Use 'Model Context Protocol' (already in list) and 'MCP server'/'MCP servers' (proposed add) instead.
All inspected hits are Hardware-in-the-Loop / SITL/HOOTL test rigs from defense, avionics, and robotics roles, not Human-in-the-Loop AI review. The acronym is unsalvageable in this corpus.

All five inspected examples are C/C++ multithreading, Node.js event-loop memory, and embedded/Linux memory management - not agent memory. Hit rate (0.0079) is dominated by classical systems engineering.
Hits are exclusively DDR4/DDR5 memory validation roles ('Validation Software Engineer - DDR5 Memory Systems'). Zero overlap with agent state.
Same as 'memory system' - DDR memory validation and 'hierarchical heterogeneous memory systems' (HPC). Not agent memory.

Two-of-three inspected hits are LSTM (Long Short-Term Memory) ML architecture references, not agent short-term memory. The hyphenated and 'long term memory' variants survive only because someone wrote 'Short term memory' without hyphen referring to LSTM.
Both inspected hits are LSTM (Long Short-Term Memory) deep learning architecture mentions, not agent memory. LSTM is the dominant 2024 meaning.
Hits are 'Cloud and Enterprise Agent team', 'SDN Agent team', 'Real Estate Agent team', 'Shopping Agent team' - human team names containing the word 'agent', not multi-agent coordination.
Single hit is 'Collaborate with platform and agent teams' meaning human teams that build agents, not coordinated agent teams. Indistinguishable from human-team usage at this volume.

guards.false_positive_pattern: Dominantly fires on job titles: 'Java architect', 'AWS Cloud Architect', 'ServiceNow architect', 'Solutions Architect', 'Enterprise Architect', 'Data Architect', etc. The 2,644 hits massively overstate architecture-as-activity.
guards.suggested_guard: Require verb-form context (e.g., 'architect and build', 'architect, design, and') OR exclude when token sits inside a job-title-style phrase ('Cloud Architect', 'Solutions Architect', '* Architect *' role line). Prefer the inflected forms 'architecting' / 'architected' which are unambiguous.

guards.false_positive_pattern: Plural overwhelmingly refers to people in titles: 'collaborate with architects', 'principal software engineers and architects', 'Enterprise architects and developers'. Almost never the verb.
guards.suggested_guard: Drop or require co-occurrence with a verb subject ('architects the system', 'architects solutions'). Otherwise a people-noun, not architecture activity.

guards.false_positive_pattern: Mostly genuine but a sizable share is 'microservices architecture'/'containerization architecture' as a stack noun rather than design activity. Also 'orchestration architecture' overlaps with the Workflow concept.
guards.suggested_guard: Accept as-is; the construct is broad by design. Optionally require co-occurrence with a design verb ('design the architecture', 'architecture decisions', 'architectural patterns') if a stricter signal is needed.

guards.false_positive_pattern: One in five inspected hits is 'A2A/B2B Integration' (legacy enterprise application-to-application integration), not Agent-to-Agent protocol. The remaining four are genuine.
guards.suggested_guard: Require co-occurrence with at least one of: 'agent', 'protocol', 'MCP', 'LangChain', 'LangGraph', 'AutoGen', 'multi-agent'. Drop matches that pair with 'B2B' or 'EDI' or 'integration scenarios'.

guards.false_positive_pattern: Includes 'Tech Breakdown | 40% Azure 40% Application Support' role-allocation tables and 'Technical Breakdown | 80% Backend' job-listing breakdowns, which are not task decomposition.
guards.suggested_guard: Require co-occurrence with 'task', 'work', 'project', 'requirements', 'problem', or 'feature'. Drop matches in tabular role-percentage contexts ('Tech Breakdown |' followed by percentages).

guards.false_positive_pattern: 'Typical task breakdown:' is boilerplate in some contractor job posts that then lists generic responsibilities, not genuine decomposition skill.
guards.suggested_guard: Accept as-is - even the boilerplate usage signals task-decomposition framing. No change required, just be aware of the duplication.

guards.false_positive_pattern: Mostly 'product roadmap' / 'technology roadmap' as a strategic-planning artifact rather than personal planning skill. Concept boundary with planning is fine but it inflates Task Decomposition's hit rate well above the actual 'breaking-down' signal.
guards.suggested_guard: Keep but acknowledge that 'roadmap' indexes strategic planning ownership rather than decomposition. Consider moving to a separate 'Strategic Planning / Roadmapping' sub-concept if granularity matters.

guards.concept: Repository Instructions and Agent Configuration Files
guards.false_positive_pattern: Three of five hits are ServiceNow 'Virtual Agent configuration' (chatbot config in ITSM) or generic 'AI Agent Configuration' UI panels - not repository instruction files like CLAUDE.md / AGENTS.md.
guards.suggested_guard: Require co-occurrence with at least one of: 'YAML', 'rules file', 'CLAUDE.md', 'AGENTS.md', '.cursorrules', 'system prompt', or 'repository'. Drop ServiceNow Virtual Agent matches.

guards.false_positive_pattern: Mostly clean in 2026-04 but at low volume; potential for LSTM contamination ('Long Short-Term Memory') if used loosely.
guards.suggested_guard: Require co-occurrence with 'agent', 'conversation', 'persistence', 'state', or 'session'. Exclude when adjacent to 'LSTM', 'RNN', or 'CNN'.

guards.false_positive_pattern: Single hit is 'seamless customer journey from chat to live agent handoff' - customer-service handoff to a human agent, not multi-agent system handoff.
guards.suggested_guard: Require co-occurrence with 'multi-agent', 'sub-agent', 'agent-to-agent', or specific frameworks (LangGraph, AutoGen). Drop customer-service / chatbot-to-human contexts.

guards.false_positive_pattern: Includes 'BDD Spec flow/Cucumber' (test framework) and 'Spec-out logging' (verb sense). Mostly genuine but the test-framework 'Spec flow' is a tool name.
guards.suggested_guard: Drop matches where 'spec' is followed by 'flow' (Cucumber SpecFlow tool). Accept verb-sense 'spec out', 'spec design' as genuine spec-authoring activity.

verification.headline_findings: The list is largely well-targeted but has a small number of catastrophic false positives that should be removed outright: 'TypeScript' (a programming language, not a static-analysis tool, 1847 hits), 'Phoenix' (overwhelmingly the city of Phoenix, AZ, 62 hits with only ~5 referring to Arize Phoenix), and 'HITL' (almost entirely 'Hardware-in-the-loop' avionics/embedded testing rather than human-in-the-loop AI). The QA/validation concept has heavy contamination from 'validation/validate/validating' against statistical model validation, requirement validation, and data validation contexts that do not signal QA-style verification expectations and need explicit guards or scoping. The AI-eval concept conflates classical performance benchmarking with LLM evaluation: 'benchmark', 'benchmarks', 'benchmarking', and 'model evaluation' fire mostly on perf benchmarking and classical ML model evaluation, not on AI output verification, dragging the concept's apparent prevalence upward. Coverage gaps: common test frameworks (JUnit, Selenium, Cypress, Jest, Playwright, Cucumber, TestRail) and concrete LLM-eval frameworks (RAGAS, TruLens, DeepEval) repeatedly appear in snippets but are absent from the list.

drops.concept: Static analysis and code quality tooling

Almost all 62 hits refer to Phoenix, AZ as a job location (or unrelated names like 'Ice Phoenix scrum team'); only a handful of snippets reference Arize Phoenix. Fix is to require disambiguation, e.g. 'Arize Phoenix'.
Of 28 hits, the dominant meaning is 'Hardware-in-the-loop' (avionics, embedded, defense test rigs); essentially none refer to human-in-the-loop AI evaluation.
Zero hits in the sample; the canonical phrase is 'LLM-as-a-judge', already in the list.
Zero hits and not commonly mentioned in SWE postings; can drop without loss.
Both observed snippets refer to 'required background checks' and 'required checks in the application' — neither is the GitHub branch-protection sense intended.
The single hit refers to REST API 'status checks (payload creation, status checks, work notes, retries)', not CI status checks.
Zero hits; would in any case collide with generic 'health/status check' usage.

guards.false_positive_pattern: Heavily fires on 'data validation', 'requirement validation', 'model validation' (statistical), 'design validation', 'validation of customer needs' — i.e., systems-engineering/data-quality validation rather than software QA. Of the top 5 snippets, 4 are non-QA contexts.
guards.suggested_guard: Require co-occurrence with software-test context: keep only when the surrounding window contains one of {test, QA, software, code, build, deploy, release, defect}; otherwise treat as out-of-topic.

guards.false_positive_pattern: Captures 'validate assumptions', 'validate hypotheses', 'validate model effectiveness', 'validate results with customer' — generic verb usage outside QA/testing.
guards.suggested_guard: Same window requirement as 'validation'; or restrict to phrases like 'validate test', 'validate functionality', 'validate fixes', 'validate code'.

guards.false_positive_pattern: Fires on 'validating data sources', 'validating statistical models', 'validating results' — research/analytics context, not software QA.
guards.suggested_guard: Require nearby test/QA/defect/code anchor.

guards.false_positive_pattern: Co-occurs heavily with systems-engineering 'verification and validation' (V&V) for hardware/defense/medical-device requirements rather than software test verification. Examples include 'design verification', 'data verification testing', 'systems integration, verification and validation, cost and risk'.
guards.suggested_guard: If matched alongside 'systems engineering', 'requirements traceability', or 'medical device', consider it systems-engineering V&V rather than software QA. Either tag separately or require software/code/test anchor in the window.

guards.false_positive_pattern: Often refers to the QA function/role (cross-functional team mention) rather than QA activity, e.g. 'product management, design, and QA' — informative but not necessarily an expectation that this engineer does QA.
guards.suggested_guard: Acceptable as is for topic prevalence, but in role-level analysis differentiate 'QA function/team' mentions from 'do QA / write tests / QA process' phrasings.

guards.false_positive_pattern: All five top snippets are performance benchmarking ('benchmark service performance', 'Benchmark systems, analyze system bottlenecks', 'setting a benchmark for engineering excellence') — not AI/model output evaluation.
guards.suggested_guard: Restrict to AI context: require nearby {LLM, model, AI, eval, prompt, RAG, agent, hallucination}; otherwise route to a perf-testing concept.

guards.false_positive_pattern: Mixed: some are LLM benchmarks ('language models against internal benchmarks'), but many are perf benchmarks ('performance indicators and metrics for key benchmarks', 'performance benchmarks').
guards.suggested_guard: Same AI-context window requirement as 'benchmark'.

guards.false_positive_pattern: Top snippets are GPU kernel benchmarking, device characterization, front-end caching benchmarking — performance work, not AI output evaluation.
guards.suggested_guard: Require AI/LLM/model context window.

guards.false_positive_pattern: Most hits are classical ML 'data preprocessing, feature engineering, and model evaluation' lists for CNN/RNN/GAN training pipelines, not LLM output verification or AI quality eval.
guards.suggested_guard: Acceptable for the broader concept of 'evaluating model output' if the concept is rescoped to include classical ML eval; otherwise require LLM/GenAI/agent/prompt anchor in window.

guards.false_positive_pattern: Same pattern as 'model evaluation'; mix of classical ML and LLM contexts.
guards.suggested_guard: Same as 'model evaluation'.

guards.false_positive_pattern: Catches HR 'performance evaluations for assigned staff' and engineering 'field evaluations / Technology Readiness Level' — non-AI senses.
guards.suggested_guard: Drop bare 'evaluations'; rely on 'model evaluations', 'LLM evaluations', 'AI evaluations', 'human evaluations' instead.

guards.false_positive_pattern: Bare verb 'monitor' fires on 'monitor security protocols', 'monitor and analyze system performance', 'monitor server-side web applications' — not specifically post-deployment observability of production behavior. Also 'glucose monitors', 'Continuous Glucose Monitors'.
guards.suggested_guard: Drop bare 'monitor'/'monitors'/'monitored' or restrict to compound forms ('monitor production', 'monitor service health', 'monitor SLO/SLA', 'monitor metrics').

guards.false_positive_pattern: Heavily catches 'business metrics' (CSAT, NPS, NDR), 'quality metrics' (in QA/testing context), 'metrics for technology products' generally — many are not post-deployment observability metrics.
guards.suggested_guard: Restrict to operational/observability metrics: require co-occurrence with {monitor, alert, dashboard, SLI, SLO, latency, error rate, throughput, OpenTelemetry, Prometheus, logging}.

guards.false_positive_pattern: Mostly 'stack trace' / 'CICS dumps and traces' / generic 'trace record' — debugging or unrelated metaphor, not artifact proof.
guards.suggested_guard: Drop bare 'trace'; keep multi-word forms 'distributed tracing', 'request trace', 'audit trace', 'execution trace'.

concept_redefines.concept: Static analysis and code quality tooling
concept_redefines.issue: Including 'TypeScript' as static analysis is a category error: it is a language, not a checker. The concept's hit rate is inflated by ~30% because of this single keyword (1847 of 2950 concept-postings include 'TypeScript').
concept_redefines.suggested_fix: Drop 'TypeScript'. If type-checking-as-verification is in scope, replace with explicit type-checking activity phrases: 'strict types', 'type safety', 'type checks', 'static types' (in addition to existing 'type checking' and 'mypy'). Recompute the concept hit rate after the drop.

concept_redefines.issue: The concept currently mixes (a) classical-ML evaluation vocabulary (benchmark/benchmarking/model evaluation) and (b) LLM/GenAI-specific evaluation (RAGAS, LangSmith, hallucination, guardrails, LLM-as-a-judge). These are distinct phenomena in the labor-market signal: (a) is decade-old DS/ML, (b) is post-2023 GenAI.
concept_redefines.suggested_fix: Either split into two sub-concepts ('Classical ML evaluation' vs 'LLM/AI output verification'), or scope the concept tightly to LLM/AI-era and require an LLM/AI/agent/prompt/GenAI anchor for ambiguous keywords (benchmark*, model evaluation*, evaluations, eval). This will give a much more accurate post-2023 GenAI signal.

concept_redefines.issue: 'validation/validate/validating/verification' are heavily polluted by systems-engineering V&V, statistical model validation, and requirements validation — domains that share the vocabulary but represent different work. This inflates the concept's hit rate disproportionately for medical-device, defense, and data-engineering postings.
concept_redefines.suggested_fix: Either (i) require a software-test anchor (test/QA/code/defect/build) within a small window for these four keywords, or (ii) split out 'systems-engineering V&V' as a separate concept so the QA concept reflects software-QA expectation specifically.

concept_redefines.issue: Bare verbs 'monitor'/'monitored'/'monitors' and the noun 'metrics' are too generic and pull in security-monitoring, business-metrics, and unrelated 'monitor server' usage. The concept's 26% hit rate is partly an artifact of these tokens.
concept_redefines.suggested_fix: Drop bare 'monitor'/'monitors'/'monitored' or restrict to compound phrases ('production monitoring', 'service monitoring', 'health monitor'). For 'metrics', require co-occurrence with operational anchors ({alert, dashboard, SLI, SLO, latency, error rate, OpenTelemetry, Prometheus, logging}).

concept_redefines.issue: The concept conflates two different activities: (a) producing artifacts as evidence of correctness (logs/screenshots/traces submitted with PRs) and (b) generic logging/tracing infrastructure (which overlaps with observability). 'logging', 'logs', 'log', 'tracing', 'traces' are heavily used in the latter sense and largely double-count with the observability concept.
concept_redefines.suggested_fix: Either narrow this concept to true artifact-as-evidence vocabulary ('reproduce', 'reproducibility', 'reproducible', 'audit trail', 'audit log', 'screenshots', 'recording', 'evidence'), moving 'logging/logs/tracing/traces' wholly to observability; or accept the overlap and document that this concept is largely co-extensive with observability infra.

mentorship.headline_findings: Direct mentorship vocabulary (mentor*, mentoring, mentorship) is solid but should be paired with a teaching-direction guard since 'mentor'/'coach' frequently target customers, end-users, partners, or non-engineers. The 'Guidance and influence' concept is severely overbroad: bare tokens like 'guidance' (2,097), 'technical guidance' (518), 'consulting' (493), 'advisor' (219), 'advocate' (318), and 'technical leadership' (1,048) overwhelmingly fire on customer/stakeholder advisory or generic seniority signals rather than peer teaching, inflating the concept hit rate to 22%. The 'Debugging and code-reading as a craft' concept is misclassified: 'debug'/'debugs'/'debugging' fire on baseline coding duties, not on debugging-as-mentorship; only the 'walkthroughs' family genuinely fits. Several concepts (Onboarding, Code review as teaching, Architecture and design guidance, Teaching) contain keywords that capture generic process language ('onboarding', 'review code', 'training materials', 'establish best practices', 'technical direction') with little or no mentorship framing. Recommend tightening the high-volume buckets via co-occurrence guards (require nearby mentor/junior/team-member/teach/grow language) and dropping or scoping ~30 keywords whose snippets show systematic false positives.

concept_redefines.concept: Debugging and code-reading as a craft
concept_redefines.issue: The bare 'debug*' family (debug/debugs/debugged/debugging) accounts for nearly all 3,414 hits and almost never carries mentorship/learning framing — it's a baseline coding-duty verb. The concept's claimed framing (debugging as a teaching activity) is not detectable from these tokens alone.
concept_redefines.suggested_fix: Either drop the concept entirely, or restrict it to bigrams that carry the teaching/learning sense: keep only 'code walkthroughs', 'walkthroughs', 'reading code', 'read code', 'debugging together', 'pair-debugging', 'walk through the code', 'live debugging session'. Drop bare debug/debugs/debugged/debugging. Without this fix the concept will dominate Mentorship hit-rate purely through generic JD verbiage.

concept_redefines.issue: Concept is presently a catch-all that conflates (a) senior IC mentoring engineers via influence with (b) customer/stakeholder advisory roles ('trusted advisor', 'consulting engineer', 'technical advisor for cloud solution') and (c) generic seniority titles ('technical leadership', 'engineering leadership', 'thought leader'). The concept hit rate (22.4%) is artificially inflated by signals unrelated to engineer development.
concept_redefines.suggested_fix: Split into two sub-concepts: (1) 'Influence-without-authority over engineers' — keep 'lead by example', 'leading by example', 'lead through influence', 'influence without authority', 'role model', 'be a role model', 'set an example', 'champion engineering', 'support junior engineers', 'guide junior', 'guide engineers', 'guide the team', 'guide others'; (2) move 'consulting', 'advisor*', 'trusted advisor', 'technical advisor', 'thought leader*', 'advocate' OUT (they belong to customer-advisory or seniority topics, not mentorship). Apply human-object guard to remaining 'guidance' phrases.

concept_redefines.issue: Most keywords in this concept ('review code', 'reviewing code', 'peer review*', 'architectural review*', 'design discussions', 'provide feedback') overwhelmingly capture review-as-correctness-gate rather than review-as-teaching, duplicating Verification signal. Definition explicitly notes the overlap but the keyword list does not enforce the disambiguation.
concept_redefines.suggested_fix: Treat the concept as 'review + teaching framing in same sentence'. Either (a) require a teaching/feedback/mentor/grow/learning co-occurrence as a hard rule on review-family tokens, or (b) keep only the explicitly teaching-marked phrases — 'thoughtful code review', 'review that teaches', 'constructive feedback' (with giver-guard), 'actionable feedback', 'thoughtful feedback', 'code review and mentorship' bigrams. Remove or guard the bare review-family tokens.

concept_redefines.issue: Bare 'onboard'/'onboarding' tokens are heavily polluted by product/data/integration onboarding ('onboarding new datasets', 'onboarding flow', 'onboard integrations', 'clients onboard', 'employee lifecycle onboarding product'). The 'first 30/90 days' tokens hit certification deadlines.
concept_redefines.suggested_fix: Require human object on all onboarding tokens: 'onboarding (new hires|engineers|developers|colleagues|team members|new team members|new employees)'. Require ramp/learn/plan/orientation context on '30/90 days' tokens; reject when followed by '...of employment to obtain certification'.

concept_redefines.issue: Top-volume 'coach'/'coaching'/'coaches' tokens fire on 'coach business stakeholders', 'coach end users', 'player/coach' metaphor, and on 'receive coaching' (the engineer is the recipient, not the giver). 'Raise the bar' is almost always quality-oriented, not engineer-oriented.
concept_redefines.suggested_fix: Apply (a) engineer-object guard to coach* tokens (engineer*|developer*|junior*|peer*|team member*|other(s)*); (b) giver-role guard (reject 'receive', 'under coaching of'); (c) require 'team' or engineer-context proximity for 'raise the bar' family. Drop 'support the growth' bare token in favor of 'support the growth of (engineers|peers|team|others|colleagues|teammates)'.

concept_redefines.issue: Half the volume comes from 'educate*', 'training sessions', 'training materials', 'deliver training', 'create training' — almost all directed at customers, end users, or business stakeholders rather than fellow engineers. The concept definition restricts to 'fellow engineers' but the keyword list does not.
concept_redefines.suggested_fix: Drop bare educate* and training* tokens. Replace with engineer-aimed bigrams only: 'tech talks', 'brown bags', 'lunch and learn', 'lunch-and-learns', 'internal presentations', 'engineering wiki', 'knowledge sharing sessions', 'tech sharing sessions', 'engineer-led training', 'technical workshops', 'share lessons learned'. Keep 'knowledge sharing', 'knowledge transfer', 'share knowledge', 'sharing knowledge' (these tend to be engineer-oriented in context).

concept_redefines.issue: Concept is mostly clean but 'collaborative development' and 'work side by side' bigrams capture generic Git/team collaboration rather than synchronous coding. 'Swarming' fires on incident-response and product names.
concept_redefines.suggested_fix: Drop 'collaborative development', 'work side by side', 'work side-by-side', 'swarming'. Add synchronous-coding variants from snippets: 'ping-pong programming', 'paired / mobbing', 'pairing and mobbing', 'pair programming sessions'.

concept_redefines.issue: Smallest concept (151 hits) and somewhat redundant with 'Code review as teaching'. 'Feedback loop' is captured here but almost always means ML/process feedback loop, not interpersonal feedback. 'Force multiplier' is half product-marketing.

performance.headline_findings: The 'performance' topic shows severe semantic drift in three concepts and good signal in the technical core. depth_claim_language is the worst offender: terms like 'fundamentals', 'fluent in', 'fluency in', 'mastery', 'expert in', 'expert level', 'subject matter expert', 'SME', 'from scratch', and 'ground up' fire on commodity job-posting boilerplate (e.g., 'mastery of JavaScript', 'fluent in Git workflows', 'building data pipelines from scratch', 'SME for upper management', 'compliance SME'), inflating the topic hit-rate to 39 percent without indicating depth-of-understanding demand. low_level_systems_programming over-includes embedded/firmware roles ('systems engineer', 'systems engineering', 'firmware', 'embedded systems', 'embedded software') which is its own labor-market category and crowds out true low-level perf work; 'assembly' and 'SSE' have catastrophic literal-match collisions (camera assembly, Systems Security Engineering). scaling_and_efficiency includes runaway commodity terms ('efficiency', 'scalability', 'highly scalable', 'efficient code') that fire on generic JD copy. By contrast, the technical concepts (latency_throughput_scale numeric framings, profiling tools, compiler/kernel internals, GPU/FPGA, lock-free, query optimization, Iceberg/compaction) work cleanly and are the topic's actual signal — the depth-claim concept should be narrowed to depth-framings only, and a 'performance-critical work / from-first-principles design' subset should be preserved.

concept_redefines.issue: The concept currently mixes three things: (a) genuine depth-of-understanding framings ('deep understanding', 'deep expertise', 'in-depth knowledge', 'first principles', 'rigorous understanding'), (b) generic skill-proficiency boilerplate ('mastery of X', 'fluent in X', 'expert in X', 'fundamentals of X'), and (c) greenfield-project framings ('from scratch', 'ground up'). All three fire on virtually every JD, inflating the topic to ~39% hit-rate without measuring the construct.
concept_redefines.suggested_fix: Restrict to category (a) only: keep 'deep understanding/knowledge/expertise/familiarity', 'in-depth understanding/knowledge', 'thorough understanding/knowledge', 'rigorous understanding', 'first principles', 'from first principles', 'first-principles', 'intimately familiar', 'intimate knowledge', 'under the hood', 'deeply understand'. Drop all (b) and (c) keywords. Consider also dropping unweighted 'solid understanding' / 'strong understanding' which are universal JD-opener phrasing — they signal nothing distinctive about depth-demand.

concept_redefines.issue: The concept conflates two labor markets: (1) low-level performance/systems work (assembly, SIMD, memory model, lock-free) which is the topic's intent, and (2) embedded/firmware engineering (medical devices, avionics, automotive) which is its own category and brings in 1500+ postings dominating the concept.
concept_redefines.suggested_fix: Either split into two concepts ('low_level_systems_programming' for SIMD/atomics/memory-model/assembly + 'embedded_firmware' separate), or drop 'firmware', 'embedded systems', 'embedded software', 'systems engineer', 'systems engineering' from this concept and rely on the precise low-level keywords. Also remove the literal-collision keywords 'assembly', 'SSE'.

concept_redefines.issue: Universal JD verbiage ('scalability', 'efficiency', 'highly scalable', 'efficient code') accounts for the bulk of the 23% concept hit rate but carries no perf-engineering signal.
concept_redefines.suggested_fix: Drop 'scalability', 'efficiency', 'highly scalable', 'efficient code'. Keep numeric/quantified forms ('scale to millions', 'scale to billions', 'massive scale', 'horizontal scaling', 'vertical scaling') and resource-specific terms ('CPU utilization', 'memory footprint', 'memory pressure', 'memory leaks', 'capacity planning'). 'Cost optimization' / 'cost efficiency' are FinOps-coded and should arguably be moved to a finops/cost concept rather than performance.

concept_redefines.issue: 'high performance' / 'high-performance' fire on culture-deck and team-naming boilerplate ('high-performance team', 'high-performance, diverse thought') as much as on actual high-performance computing.
concept_redefines.suggested_fix: Keep precise variants 'high-performance computing' and 'HPC' only; drop bare 'high performance' / 'high-performance' OR guard them by requiring co-occurrence with 'computing', 'system', 'application', 'database', 'code' within +/- 5 tokens (excluding 'team', 'culture', 'environment').

concept_redefines.issue: Generic terms ('consensus', 'replication', 'partitioning') fire heavily on non-internals usage (stakeholder consensus, ETL replication, table partitioning). Meanwhile half the keywords are zero-hit theoretical-CS terms (linearizability, CAP theorem, vector clocks, CRDT singular form, MVCC, LSM tree).
concept_redefines.suggested_fix: For 'consensus' and 'replication', require neighboring algorithmic context ('Raft', 'Paxos', 'log', 'leader', 'quorum') OR drop them. Drop or merge zero-hit theoretical terms: keep the few that do fire ('CRDTs', 'consistency models', 'two-phase commit', 'WAL') and consider that this concept is a low-recall by-design signal.

concept_redefines.issue: Bare 'compiler' / 'compilers' / 'code generation' have shifted in meaning: 'compiler' often means 'C++ compiler flags' toolchain mention, and 'code generation' now frequently means 'GitHub Copilot AI code generation'.
concept_redefines.suggested_fix: Drop bare 'compiler', 'compilers', 'code generation'. Rely on 'compiler internals', 'compiler optimization(s)', 'LLVM', 'MLIR', 'JIT compilation', 'AOT compilation', 'codegen' (which in the data appears mainly in backend-compiler context), 'operator fusion', 'optimization passes', 'JVM tuning', 'JVM internals'.

concept_redefines.issue: Iceberg/compaction/execution-plan are data-engineering surface-area, not engine-internals. The concept overlaps heavily with data-warehouse tooling.
concept_redefines.suggested_fix: Drop 'Iceberg', 'compaction', 'execution plan' from this concept (they belong to a data-engineering concept if needed). Keep 'database internals', 'storage engine(s)', 'query optimizer', 'PostgreSQL internals', 'MySQL internals', 'InnoDB' for the internals signal even though hit-counts are low.

concept_redefines.issue: 'Metal' has catastrophic literal-match collision (sheet metal, bare metal hosting). 'RTL' collides with React Testing Library. 'HIP' is an acronym fragile. The concept also splits awkwardly between GPU programming (CUDA, cuDNN, ROCm) and FPGA/ASIC hardware design (Verilog, VHDL).
concept_redefines.suggested_fix: Drop 'Metal', 'RTL', 'HIP'. Consider splitting into 'gpu_programming' (CUDA, GPU kernels, cuDNN, Triton, Vulkan, Metal-API guarded) and 'hardware_design_internals' (FPGA, ASIC, Verilog, SystemVerilog, VHDL, microarchitecture, cache hierarchy, NUMA).

concept_redefines.issue: 'tracing' and 'instrumentation' fire on regulatory/industrial usage; 'benchmark' singular fires figuratively.
concept_redefines.suggested_fix: Replace 'tracing' with 'distributed tracing'; require 'instrumentation' to co-occur with metrics/observability/profiling terms; drop 'benchmark' singular in favor of 'benchmarks'/'benchmarking'.

concept_redefines.issue: 'data structures' is universal hiring-bar language with no algorithmic-optimization signal. Half of the concept's keywords are zero-hit (Big O variants, asymptotic complexity).
concept_redefines.suggested_fix: Drop 'data structures'. Keep specific complexity language ('time complexity', 'space complexity', 'computational complexity', 'complexity analysis', 'algorithm optimization', 'numerical optimization', 'numerical methods'). Consider that this concept's true positive rate is small; it may be best merged into performance_optimization_general.

process_scaffolding.headline_findings: The process_scaffolding list is structurally sound for the head terms (agile/scrum/sprint/requirements/specifications/SDLC/governance/coordination/scheduling/Jira), but it is contaminated by a cluster of short ambiguous tokens that match unrelated tech (SAFe -> 'safe', LeSS -> 'less' / LESS CSS preprocessor, SM -> AWS Secrets Manager, PO -> SAP/Oracle Purchase Order, NFR -> the company NFR, ART -> 'state of the art', PLC -> Programmable Logic Controller, XP -> Windows XP, TPM -> Trusted Platform Module, Linear -> 'linear algebra', PM -> '5 PM' / Portfolio Manager, CSM -> ServiceNow Customer Service Management, CAB -> 'Cab Engineering', CPM -> Cost-Per-Mille). A second cluster of V&V terms (validate/validating/validates/validated, verify/verifying/verifies/verified) fires overwhelmingly on data-validation, model-validation, and input-validation snippets rather than process-level V&V. A third cluster of acronym-only forms (DoD, V&V, IV&V, RTM, SRS, ERD, PMI) is largely on-topic but DoD has a heavy second meaning (Department of Defense clearance) that dominates its hits. Finally, the list is missing a small number of mainstream additions visible in snippets (DOORS, PI Planning evidence supports adding 'release train engineer'/'RTE', 'IMS'/'integrated master schedule', 'IMP', 'CMDB', 'Six Sigma', 'agilist', 'grooming').

concept_redefines.issue: The bare verb forms (validate, validating, validates, validated, verify, verifying, verifies, verified, validation) are dominated by data-validation, model-validation, and input-validation usage in DS/ML/backend postings, not process-level V&V. As currently defined the concept double-counts a generic engineering activity that is not the systems-engineering V&V of the topic definition.
concept_redefines.suggested_fix: Either (a) move the bare verb forms to a guarded list that requires co-occurrence with 'requirements', 'system', 'design', 'qualification', 'IV&V', 'V&V', 'verification', or 'process' within the same sentence/window; or (b) split the concept into 'Process-level V&V' (qualification testing, IV&V, V&V, system verification, design verification, validation plan, qualification test) vs. 'General verify/validate verbs' (which probably should be dropped from process_scaffolding entirely as too generic).

concept_redefines.issue: Two-letter acronym keywords (SM, PO) collide with unrelated SAP/Oracle/AWS terminology and add hundreds of false positives across the broader topic; CSM collides with ServiceNow Customer Service Management which is a strong false-positive cluster.
concept_redefines.suggested_fix: Drop 'SM' and 'PO' outright. For 'CSM', require co-occurrence with 'scrum'/'agile'/'certified'/'master' in same sentence; otherwise the ServiceNow CSM module dominates.

concept_redefines.concept: Kanban / Lean / Waterfall and other methodologies
concept_redefines.issue: 'SAFe' is duplicated here and in 'Agile methodology' (557 hits each, presumably the same hits), and 'LeSS' captures CSS preprocessor usage rather than Large-Scale Scrum.
concept_redefines.suggested_fix: Deduplicate 'SAFe' across the two concepts (decide which concept owns it). Apply case-sensitive matching for both 'SAFe' and 'LeSS' with required context words ('framework', 'scrum', 'agile', 'release train').

concept_redefines.issue: 'ART' (515 hits) and 'PLC' (110 hits) are three-letter tokens whose dominant meanings ('state of the art' and 'Programmable Logic Controller') are unrelated to process governance and inflate the concept by ~600 postings.
concept_redefines.suggested_fix: Drop both. The intended SAFe Agile Release Train meaning is already captured by 'agile release train' / 'release train' / 'release trains'. The Product Life Cycle meaning is already captured by 'product lifecycle' / 'product life cycle'.

concept_redefines.issue: Two- and three-letter role acronyms (PM, TPM, TPMs) carry severe semantic collisions (clock time, Trusted Platform Module, Technical Performance Measures) and 'Linear' as a tool name collides with 'linear algebra' which dominates DS/ML postings.
concept_redefines.suggested_fix: Drop 'PM', 'TPM', 'TPMs', 'Linear'. Spelled-out forms ('project manager', 'project managers', 'technical program manager', 'technical program managers', 'PMs') already cover the role meanings cleanly.

legacy_stack.headline_findings: The vocabulary is broadly well-targeted at legacy enterprise stacks but suffers from several short-token false positives that fire on unrelated text: 'RPG' matches video-game RPG, 'IMS' matches the staffing firm 'IMS' and 'CMS/IMS', 'WAS' matches the past-tense English verb almost universally, 'VMS' matches generic plural 'VMs'/'cloud VMs' rather than OpenVMS, 'CVS' matches the pharmacy/retailer CVS, 'Hudson' matches place names (New Hudson, Hudson River Trading), and 'Ant' matches the proper noun ANT in deployment toolchains plus 'Salesforce ant' which is itself fine but 'Ant' on its own is too noisy without word boundaries. Several keywords are not actually 'legacy' by 2024 standards and contaminate the topic: 'SOAP', 'WSDL', 'XSD', 'SOA', 'Service Oriented Architecture', 'LDAP', 'Active Directory', 'Kerberos', 'MuleSoft', 'Group Policy', 'Perforce', 'Ant', 'PL/SQL', 'Teradata', 'DB2', 'Hyper-V', and '.NET Framework' all fire heavily in modern hybrid/cloud JDs and on Salesforce/ServiceNow/AWS roles. The most reliable concepts are 'Mainframe languages and platforms' (COBOL/JCL/CICS/VSAM/REXX cluster cleanly), 'Legacy Microsoft .NET stack' specific markers (WebForms/WCF/VB.NET/Classic ASP/WPF), and 'Legacy Java EE / app server stack' core (J2EE/EJB/WebLogic/WebSphere/JBoss/Struts/JSP). New legacy mainframe markers visible in snippets but missing from the vocabulary include IDMS, ENDEVOR, TSO/ISPF, File Aid, Xpediter, Abend Aid, ACOB/UCOB, CLIST, and IBM ECM/Content Navigator; Oracle Forms variants and Oracle ADF/Webcenter are also undercaptured. Recommended structural fix: split the SOAP/SOA concept into a 'mainframe-and-truly-legacy SOAP' subset versus 'still-current REST+SOAP integration' which should be excluded, and move modern items (MuleSoft, Active Directory, LDAP, Kerberos, Group Policy) out of legacy-stack into integration/identity topics.

drops.reason: Bare 'RPG' matches video-game role-playing-game references (action RPG, MMO RPG elements) far more than IBM RPG language; specific variants RPGLE/RPG/400/RPG IV are sufficient.

False positives: snippet 'IMS is currently offering an exciting opportunity' (staffing/consulting firm name); also matches CMS/IMS healthcare and 'IMS Health'. Use 'IMS DB' or 'IMS/DB' instead, which appears in true mainframe snippets.

drops.concept: Legacy Java EE / app server stack
drops.reason: WebSphere Application Server abbreviation collides with the English past-tense verb 'was'; all 5 sampled examples are unrelated prose ('was named one of', 'was completed', 'was intended'). Drop in favor of 'WebSphere Application Server'.
Bare 'VMS' overwhelmingly matches plural 'VMs' (virtual machines) in modern Azure/AWS contexts ('cloud VMs', 'guest VMs', 'Windows VMs'), not OpenVMS. Use 'OpenVMS' or 'VAX/VMS' only.

Frequent collision with CVS Health/CVS Pharmacy as employer; mainframe context is rare. Even when meant as version control it's grouped with git/svn as a generic ancient mention rather than indicating legacy SDLC.

Matches place names ('New Hudson, MI'), 'Hudson River Trading' (HFT employer), and is otherwise listed only alongside Jenkins as an alternative; rarely indicates an actual legacy Hudson CI deployment.
3-letter token: matches 'ANT' in modern Salesforce DX/Force.com and Maven/Ant toolchains, plus 'ant' (insect) and Salesforce 'Ant Migration Tool' which is current. Not a reliable legacy signal.
Apache Ant is still widely used alongside Maven/Jenkins in current Java builds; not a legacy marker on its own.

MuleSoft Anypoint Platform is a current Salesforce-owned iPaaS heavily used in cloud-native API-led integration; 116 hits are dominated by modern Salesforce/ETL contexts. Move to integration topic, not legacy.

582 hits but virtually all are 'REST and/or SOAP', 'SOAP/REST APIs' in modern third-party-integration JDs (ServiceNow, Salesforce, Java microservices). Bare SOAP is not a legacy marker — only SOAP-only stacks are.
Co-occurs with REST/JSON/Swagger in current integration JDs; supporting SOAP+WSDL is still required in finance/healthcare integration roles and does not by itself signal pre-2015 legacy.
XSD/XML Schema is used in any current XML-based system (HL7, FpML, FIX, Oracle BPEL); fires on modern .NET Core 5+ JDs. Not a legacy signal on its own.
234 hits; sampled snippets show modern usage 'microservices, SOA, and event-driven architecture', AWS/Azure/GCP architects listing SOA as a paradigm. Not a legacy signal.
Same FP pattern as SOA — appears as an architectural-pattern keyword in current solution-architect JDs alongside microservices and cloud.

Most snippets say '.NET Framework 4.x and newer versions, ASP.NET/C#, .NET Core' — i.e., the JD is listing .NET Framework as one stack alongside .NET Core/.NET 6+. Without a version constraint (4.x and below) this is not a legacy marker. Replace with version-pinned variants ('.NET Framework 4.5/4.6/4.7/4.8') and rely on '.NET Framework' only as a co-occurrence requirement.
Both observed snippets use 'Dotnet framework' as a generic synonym for '.NET' in QA/test-automation JDs (testing modern RESTful APIs); not a legacy signal.
Bare 'Domino' matches Domino's Pizza ('Domino's @Retail ecosystem') and 'Domino Data Labs' (modern ML platform). Restrict to 'Lotus Domino' / 'Notes Domino' which already exist in the list.

guards.false_positive_pattern: Possible 3-letter collisions, but sampled snippets are clean ('Mvs Cobol', 'MVS JCL', 'CICS, MVS, ISPF'). One snippet 'MVS, VIP, RSI' is ambiguous.
guards.suggested_guard: Require co-occurrence with mainframe context tokens (z/OS, JCL, COBOL, CICS, ISPF, OS/390) within +/-50 chars to be safe; otherwise it's mostly fine.

guards.false_positive_pattern: 3-letter token. Sampled hits are all VMware ESX, but 'ESX' could match ESXi tail or proper-noun strings.
guards.suggested_guard: Require word boundary and either 'VMware' within +/-30 chars or membership in a hypervisor list; current data is clean but the surface area is risky.

guards.false_positive_pattern: Risk of matching as a substring (titles like 'Software Engineer I, AIX'); current sample is clean and refers to IBM AIX UNIX.
guards.suggested_guard: Word-boundary regex \bAIX\b only; keep.

guards.false_positive_pattern: Clean in sample; sometimes appears in 'Oracle Solaris (UNIX)' lists alongside Linux/Windows in security-clearance JDs as a legacy-friendly skill rather than current focus.
guards.suggested_guard: Keep as-is; high precision.

guards.false_positive_pattern: Clean; TFS is unambiguously Team Foundation Server. Note many snippets describe migrating away from TFS to Azure DevOps — which is itself a legacy-stack signal.
guards.suggested_guard: Keep; consider weighting 'migrating TFS' contexts as strong legacy-maintenance evidence.

guards.false_positive_pattern: Heavily co-occurs with 'Azure AD'/'Entra'/cloud IAM in modern hybrid-cloud JDs; not pre-cloud per se. Many snippets are cloud-architect roles integrating with on-prem AD.
guards.suggested_guard: Require absence of 'Azure AD'/'Entra'/'Okta' co-occurrence, OR require co-occurrence with 'on-prem' / 'Domain Controller' / 'Group Policy'. Otherwise demote weight.

guards.false_positive_pattern: Modern Ping Identity, ForgeRock, Hadoop/Cloudera, and security-engineer JDs mention LDAP as a current protocol; not legacy per se.
guards.suggested_guard: Demote to weak signal; require co-occurrence with mainframe/legacy-AD context for legacy classification.

guards.false_positive_pattern: Listed alongside SAML/OAuth/JWT/SSO in modern security-engineer JDs; current Kafka/Hadoop/SRE roles routinely require it.
guards.suggested_guard: Same as LDAP — demote, or require legacy co-context.

guards.false_positive_pattern: Common in current Windows-centric DoD/clearance JDs alongside cloud and modern PowerShell. Not specifically legacy.
guards.suggested_guard: Demote weight or require on-prem AD co-occurrence.

guards.false_positive_pattern: Hyper-V is current Microsoft hypervisor still shipping with Windows Server 2022/Azure Stack; not strictly legacy.
guards.suggested_guard: Treat as 'on-prem virtualization' rather than legacy; or require co-occurrence with VMware/ESX/Citrix.

guards.false_positive_pattern: 268 hits — Oracle PL/SQL is still widely used in current Oracle-cloud and on-prem Oracle DB jobs; appears in cloud-ready/Docker JDs.
guards.suggested_guard: Demote; require co-occurrence with another legacy marker (Forms, EBS, WebLogic, mainframe) or Oracle pre-12c.

guards.false_positive_pattern: Teradata Vantage is a current cloud DW; appears alongside Snowflake/Redshift/BigQuery in modern data-engineer JDs.
guards.suggested_guard: Demote; not a clean legacy marker without 'Teradata 13/14/15' context.

guards.false_positive_pattern: Mostly listed in 'Oracle, SQL Server, DB2, Teradata' enumerations in current data-warehouse/data-engineer JDs; not specifically legacy unless DB2 for z/OS.
guards.suggested_guard: Use 'DB2 for z/OS' / 'DB2/400' subvariants for high-precision legacy; keep 'DB2' but at lower weight.

guards.false_positive_pattern: Perforce Helix is current and dominant in game dev / large binary asset teams (Unity/UE5). Not a legacy SDLC signal in 2024.
guards.suggested_guard: Demote or move out of legacy; or require co-occurrence with non-game industry context.

adds.concept: Legacy Java EE / app server stack
adds.evidence_snippet: Replaces ambiguous 'WAS' acronym; standard expansion appears in many JDs and is unambiguous.

concept_redefines.issue: The concept conflates 'a JD that mentions SOAP' (overwhelmingly modern integration roles requiring REST AND SOAP) with 'pre-REST SOAP-only legacy stack'. Hit rate of 3% on SOAP alone wildly overstates legacy presence.
concept_redefines.suggested_fix: Reframe as 'pre-REST SOAP-only stack' and require absence of REST/RESTful/JSON-API in the same JD, OR require co-occurrence of >=2 SOAP-era markers (e.g., WSDL+CXF, JAX-WS+Axis, BPEL+OSB). Drop bare 'SOAP', 'WSDL', 'XSD' as standalone signals.

concept_redefines.issue: Mixes truly legacy ESBs (TIBCO BW, webMethods, BizTalk, Oracle SOA Suite, Mule ESB community edition) with modern iPaaS/cloud (MuleSoft Anypoint, current Kafka-adjacent ESB framings) and with the architectural pattern 'SOA' itself.
concept_redefines.suggested_fix: Drop 'SOA' and 'Service Oriented Architecture' as keywords (they describe a pattern, not a legacy product). Move 'MuleSoft' to a separate 'integration platforms' topic. Keep TIBCO/webMethods/BizTalk/IBM MQ/MQSeries as legacy.

concept_redefines.issue: Active Directory, LDAP, Kerberos, Group Policy are all current technologies in hybrid-cloud and security-clearance JDs. Treating them as legacy markers contaminates the topic with cloud-architect and modern-IAM roles.
concept_redefines.suggested_fix: Restrict the concept to clearly-deprecated identity products (Tivoli, SiteMinder, ADFS-on-prem). Move AD/LDAP/Kerberos/Group Policy to a 'hybrid-cloud / on-prem identity' topic, not 'legacy'.

concept_redefines.issue: DB2, Teradata, PL/SQL, Sybase ASE are still actively shipped/supported and appear in modern cloud-DW JDs. PL/SQL alone is one of the most-used DB skills today.
concept_redefines.suggested_fix: Define 'legacy' more strictly: require version-pinning (DB2 for z/OS, Oracle <=11g, SQL Server <=2008, Sybase ASE <=15) or co-occurrence with a mainframe/on-prem context. Drop bare PL/SQL as a legacy marker.

concept_redefines.issue: Includes Perforce (current dominant VCS in game dev), Hudson (often a generic Jenkins-alternative mention), Ant (current Java build tool), and CVS (frequent collisions with CVS Health). The concept's hit rate is inflated by FPs.
concept_redefines.suggested_fix: Restrict to ClearCase, PVCS, Visual SourceSafe/VSS, TFVC, MKS, StarTeam — pre-Git enterprise VCS. Drop Perforce and Ant entirely; keep CVS only with surrounding-context guard (within X chars of 'svn'/'subversion'/'version control'). GNU Make and Apache Ant are not legacy.

concept_redefines.issue: VBA fires at 0.26% mostly on Excel-VBA in trading/finance JDs. Excel VBA is current ('developing in Excel VBA'), not a legacy-system marker. Python 2 / Perl 5 are correct legacy markers.
concept_redefines.suggested_fix: Either drop VBA or split into 'Excel VBA' (not legacy) vs 'Access VBA / classic VB6 conversion' (legacy). Consider adding ColdFusion/CFML to a separate concept since they're a distinct legacy web stack.

concept_redefines.issue: Bare '.NET Framework' fires on JDs that list both .NET Framework and .NET Core/.NET 6+ — this is a modern hybrid posture, not legacy. WebForms, WCF, VB.NET, Classic ASP, WPF, WinForms are clean legacy markers.
concept_redefines.suggested_fix: Drop bare '.NET Framework'. Require version-pinning ('.NET Framework 4.5/4.6/4.7/4.8') OR co-occurrence with at least one specific legacy-MS marker (WebForms, Classic ASP, VB.NET, WCF, ASMX) for legacy classification.

context_infrastructure.headline_findings: The vocabulary is dominated by a few generic anchor terms that drive most of the topic's hit rate but capture phenomena other than 'context infrastructure': 'monitoring' / 'monitor' / 'metric(s)' / 'logging' / 'logs' fire on generic ops/security/business-metric language, and 'cross-functional' / 'cross-functional teams' / 'business requirements' / 'business needs' / 'written communication' fire on boilerplate filler that any posting contains. Several keywords are flatly wrong-concept ('observable' = adjective for systems, not telemetry; 'spans' = verb meaning 'covers'; 'notion' = the noun, not the SaaS; 'design reviews'/'design review' = code/electrical/security reviews, not ADR/RFC artifacts; 'ERD' = data-modeling diagrams, not architecture decision records; 'SOP'/'SOPs' = manufacturing/QA procedures, not engineering runbooks; 'business value'/'KPI'/'business outcomes'/'business impact' = generic motivational language, not product literacy; 'product knowledge' = product-line domain knowledge of what the company sells, not product-sense; 'developer experience' = years of dev experience as often as the DX discipline; 'DX' = Salesforce DX in the vast majority of hits). The Architecture-decision-records concept and the Runbooks concept are particularly contaminated by their dominant keyword: drop 'design reviews'/'design review' and 'operational excellence' and the concepts collapse to honest, low-frequency ADR/runbook signal. There are a few real omissions in the snippets ('golden signals', 'golden path', 'service catalog', 'incident review', 'distributed tracing' co-occurring with 'three pillars of observability', 'BRD', 'business requirements document', 'C4 diagrams') but most adds are modest. Overall the topic needs aggressive guarding/dropping of about 25-30 keywords before its hit rates can be trusted as a context-infrastructure signal rather than a mixture of ops, security, manufacturing-QA, and HR-boilerplate signals.

drops.reason: Verb fires on generic 'monitor server-side applications', 'monitor security protocols', 'monitor cloud privacy' — captures security/sysadmin/business activity, not telemetry stack.
Hits include 'continuous glucose monitors', 'monitors and evaluates performance', 'monitors the external environment' — not telemetry.
All snippets show the adjective ('scalable, observable systems'). It is a property claim, not a telemetry artifact; redundant with 'observability'.
Every snippet uses 'spans' as a verb ('our work spans integration and deployment', 'firmware spans multiple target classes'). Zero distributed-tracing usage observed.
Singular form fires on 'metric definitions', 'metric stakeholders', 'built metric calculators' — business/product-metric language; redundant with 'metrics' if guarded.
All snippets refer to LLM/agent context engineering (prompt/state design), not engineering documentation.
Many hits use the English word 'notion' ('strong notion of security best practices', 'the notion of having hundreds of participants'); guard cost > signal value at n=12.
Single-hit acronym; in other contexts means 'task scheduling groups' or 'technical service groups'. Not worth the noise.

Dominant keyword (n=621) but snippets show electrical/manufacturing design reviews, security design reviews, and generic code/solution reviews — only a small fraction are ADR-style architecture deliberation.
Same false-positive pattern as 'design reviews': electrical design review, threat-modeling design review, code review pairing.
Entity-Relationship Diagrams are a data-modeling artifact, not an architecture decision record. Belongs (if anywhere) under data-pipeline hygiene or system understanding.
First example is 'Request for Change' (ITIL ticketing), not 'Request for Comments'. Acronym ambiguity at low volume — drop in favor of 'RFCs' / 'request for comments'.
Dominant keyword (n=386) but the phrase is corporate boilerplate / values-language ('passion for operational excellence'); does not denote runbook authoring.
Snippets are manufacturing/compliance SOPs (ISO 9001, requirements tracing per department SOPs), not engineering runbooks.
Same as 'SOPs': QMS / ISO 13485 / Standard Operation Procedures for manufacturing — wrong domain.
Same false-positive pattern; phrase is dominantly used in manufacturing/regulated-industry contexts.
Same as plural — manufacturing SOP language, not engineering runbooks.
Generic boilerplate ('translate business requirements into technical solutions') appears in nearly every SWE posting; does not distinguish product literacy from generic dev work.
Same boilerplate pattern — 'translate user and business needs into functional code'; near-synonym for 'requirements' filler.
Generic 'deliver business value' filler; rarely indicates product/customer literacy.
Generic motivational phrase ('drive business impact', 'team and business impact through high personal output'); not product literacy.
Same generic-aspirational pattern as 'business value' / 'business impact'.
Many hits mean 'years of developer experience' (resume requirement language) rather than the DX discipline of API/portal/docs design.

guards.false_positive_pattern: Generic verb across security ('security monitoring'), sysadmin patch management, M&S team activity reports, business case monitoring.
guards.suggested_guard: Require co-occurrence with one of: alerting, logging, dashboards, observability, telemetry, metrics, traces, Prometheus, Datadog, Grafana, Splunk, ELK, New Relic, CloudWatch, SLO, OpenTelemetry — or limit to compounds 'application monitoring', 'service monitoring', 'system monitoring'.

guards.false_positive_pattern: Hits on 'business metrics like CSAT, NPS, NDR', 'quality metrics', 'reliability and performance metrics' — captures product/QA metrics, not telemetry.
guards.suggested_guard: Require co-occurrence with logs/traces/dashboards/observability/alerts/Prometheus/Datadog/Grafana, OR exclude when preceded by 'business', 'quality', 'KPI', 'success'.

guards.false_positive_pattern: Generic ('inspecting application logs', 'logs and reproduction steps in defect tracking'); also 'change logs' / 'audit logs' contexts.
guards.suggested_guard: Require co-occurrence with metrics OR traces OR observability OR aggregation/centralized/structured.

guards.false_positive_pattern: Often appears as a feature of CI/CD or generic 'best practices for logging, monitoring, error handling' boilerplate.
guards.suggested_guard: Require either a tool (Datadog, Splunk, ELK, Loki) OR a qualifier (centralized, structured, distributed, aggregation) within the same window.

guards.false_positive_pattern: Snowflake is a data warehouse product but also generic SQL skill demand — fires on resumes that just list it as a checkbox skill rather than discussing pipeline hygiene.
guards.suggested_guard: Acceptable as-is for the data-warehousing concept, but document that this is a tool-mention proxy, not a hygiene/quality signal. Consider downweighting versus 'data quality' / 'data validation' for the hygiene interpretation.

guards.false_positive_pattern: Same as snowflake — tool checkbox.
guards.suggested_guard: Same downweighting note; this is technology-stack signal, not hygiene practice signal.

guards.false_positive_pattern: Tool-checkbox listing alongside Azure stack.
guards.suggested_guard: Same as snowflake/redshift.

guards.false_positive_pattern: Boilerplate ('Collaborate with cross-functional teams' appears in ~17% of postings) — no specific coordination work described.
guards.suggested_guard: Keep but document as boilerplate filler; consider weighting only when paired with a named function (product, design, PM, marketing, legal, support, sales, data) rather than the bare phrase.

guards.false_positive_pattern: Same boilerplate as above; literal phrase.
guards.suggested_guard: Same — pair with a function-name within ±15 tokens.

guards.false_positive_pattern: Almost always part of generic 'excellent verbal and written communication skills' resume boilerplate, not technical-writing craft.
guards.suggested_guard: Require co-occurrence with technical/clear/excellent + a writing-artifact noun (documentation, design doc, RFC, runbook), OR exclude when adjacent to 'verbal'.

guards.false_positive_pattern: Hits on 'ability to write SQL queries', 'ability to write robust code in Python' — coding-skill phrasing, not writing craft.
guards.suggested_guard: Require the next noun to be in {documentation, technical, design, clearly, design documents, specs, technical documents}; exclude when followed by SQL/code/Python/queries.

guards.false_positive_pattern: Hits include 'SQL query writing skills', 'XML Writing Skills'.
guards.suggested_guard: Require preceded by 'technical' OR 'business' OR 'documentation'; exclude SQL/query/code modifiers.

guards.false_positive_pattern: Singular acronym hits include 'Azure Cloud is being used by KPI' (company name), 'KPI workshop', 'KPI objectives' as generic targets.
guards.suggested_guard: Require company-name disambiguation OR pair with 'metrics'/'targets'/'dashboards'; or fold all signal into 'KPIs' (plural is cleaner).

guards.false_positive_pattern: Vast majority of hits are 'Salesforce DX' (a Salesforce dev-tool suite), not Developer Experience.
guards.suggested_guard: Require not preceded by 'Salesforce'; OR require co-occurrence with 'developer experience' / 'devex' / 'internal developer platform'.
